response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience and genuine open-source credentials, but the role's hard requirements expose critical gaps. Most disqualifying is the German language requirement (minimum C1): the resume explicitly lists German as 'Basic,' which is incompatible with client-facing consulting and team leadership in a German-speaking market. Beyond language, the candidate's career track is consistently that of an individual contributor — even 'Staff' roles at Altom and re:cinq are engineering IC positions, not people-management or team-lead roles. There is no evidence of leading a team, owning project delivery end-to-end, or managing client relationships. UML and formal requirements management, both called out in the JD, are absent from the resume entirely.
strengths: Broad, deep technical range across stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, C/C++, embedded, mobile, GIS) spanning 15+ years, demonstrating genuine adaptability
Sustained open-source commitment (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial, vibe.dav) with public talks at FOSS4G — directly aligned with the role's open-source/Linux focus
Knowledge-sharing culture evident through workshops, code katas, and Selenium courses — positive signal for the mentoring and consulting dimensions of the role
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — a hard disqualifier against the stated C1 minimum for a client-advisory, German-market consulting role
No demonstrated team leadership, headcount management, or project ownership at the delivery level; all roles are individual contributor positions regardless of 'Staff' title
No mention of UML, formal IT architecture modeling, requirements management, or compliance/risk frameworks — core competencies explicitly listed in the job description

key_factors.factor: German language gap (Basic vs. C1 required)
Strong open-source and Linux/Unix technical background
